pages.title: 忍: delay damage
pages.text: $(l)忍$() takes final damage after vanilla armor, resistance, and similar reductions, then moves part of it into an ink wound pool.$(br2)Ink wound is delayed damage, shown with black-heart feedback. You only take the remaining portion immediately. If you go 2 seconds without another hit, the pool starts paying out; more hits keep adding to it and push the payout back.

pages.title: Tier feel
pages.text: 忍 lasts a long time, so it is good to write before caves or challenges.$(br2)Wood delays about a quarter of the damage. Iron delays more than half. Netherite delays most of it.$(br2)The ink wound pool has a cap, so weak staves cannot delay infinite debt.

pages.title: 净: clean up pressure
pages.text: $(l)净$() clears negative effects and removes a fixed amount of ink wound. Wood clears 4 ink wound and 1 negative effect; diamond clears 18 and up to 4 effects; netherite clears 22 and up to 5.$(br2)It is not always a full reset. It is the tool you keep writing when pressure remains after 忍 or 救.
